title: README first touch leads with memory-you-gain, not enforcement: advisory-by-default is the headline reassurance, one moment of teeth max
context: Owner feedback on the hero: 'a lot of block and breaks and scary things' — first-time users met ⛔ BLOCKED / VIOLATED / meltdown before seeing what they get. The strongest reassurance wasn't even stated: DEFAULT_FIRMNESS is advisory — Hunch never blocks anything until the user opts in.
decision: Rewrote the README opening: hero leads with 'your repo remembers why' and keeping AI sessions consistent with your own decisions; the gate appears as a safety net with a receipt, not a police action. Quickstart says 'advisory by default — nothing blocks until you say so' on the init line and demos plain `hunch conform` (--strict mentioned as the when-you're-ready step). The killer scenario stays (it IS the product) but as 'quietly flags it … you decide what happens next'; exactly one moment of teeth (strict, labeled opt-in). Benchmark numbers stay (verifiable) reframed from 'even Opus ignored you' to 'reminders help; the check catches what reminders miss'. Competitor names dropped from the hero in favor of 'pattern tools' (tightens compliance with the verifiable-properties stance). Rule for future edits: benefit first, receipts over threats, blocking always described as opt-in.
alternatives_rejected: Keeping the ⛔ BLOCKED hero for punch: converts the already-convinced, bounces the first-timer the README exists for
Removing the gate story entirely: the deterministic catch IS the differentiator — soften the framing, never the substance
Naming Semgrep/SonarQube in the hero contrast: category-level 'pattern tools' says the same verifiable thing with less spice (consistent with the positioning decision)
